authoritative_sources.title: Pennsylvania Medical Marijuana Program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Pennsylvania program guidance establishing that the program uses patient and practitioner registries, a seed-to-sale electronic tracking system, and strict confidentiality for patient, caregiver, practitioner, and ETS data.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Program-model grounding, registry and ETS dependency definition, and complaint-path identification
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to regulated medical-cannabis dispensary operations and requires registry and ETS-backed evidence before the lane can resolve order, pickup, or complaint work.

authoritative_sources.title: 28 Pa. Code Chapter 1161a, Dispensaries
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary dispensary regulation covering card verification, patient certification review, quantity limits, licensed-medical consultation availability, receipt filing, labels, complaints, recalls, inventory, and ETS integration.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Dispensing, order-release, complaint, recall, and traceability controls
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Provides the core refusal triggers for unsupported quantity, form, delivery, and recall exceptions and grounds the lane in specific patient, product, and receipt artifacts.

authoritative_sources.title: Medical Marijuana Patients
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official patient and caregiver workflow guidance for registry enrollment, patient certification, card payment and issuance, caregiver eligibility, background checks, and pickup prerequisites.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Patient or caregiver intake, identity grounding, and pickup-readiness checks
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps first-visit readiness, caregiver linkage, and missing-ID scenarios grounded in patient-program rules instead of generic retail account logic.

authoritative_sources.title: The Medical Marijuana Assistance Program (MMAP) Dispensary FAQs, Phase 3 and Phase 3 Pilot
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official dispensary workflow FAQ for identifying MMAP-eligible patients at the point of sale, applying benefits, using the state support tool, and handling reimbursement and discount interaction.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Pricing, discount, and point-of-sale handling for eligible patients
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes the lane specific to regulated patient pricing support instead of generic discount handling and creates evidence expectations for MMAP, POS, and reimbursement-related cases.

authoritative_sources.title: ASTM D8233-19, Standard Guide for Packaging and Labeling of Cannabis/Hemp Raw Materials and Finished Goods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized ASTM D37 retail-environment guide for cannabis packaging and labeling used as a vendor-neutral reference for patient-facing product-information and label terminology.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds cannabis retail terminology discipline so outputs reference labels, packaging, and patient-facing product information rather than generic SKU and price language.

authoritative_sources.title: ASTM D8286-19, Standard Guide for Processing Cannabis Product Complaints
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized ASTM D37 control guide defining minimum requirements to receive, process, investigate, document, and correct cannabis product complaints with corrective and preventive action expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Complaint intake, investigation support, documentation, and corrective-action routing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from closing complaints or adverse-event reports as ordinary service requests without the documentation and escalation structure expected in regulated cannabis operations.

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and patient or caregiver grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm patient, caregiver, facility, jurisdiction, order, and product identifiers and establish whether the request is informational, pricing-sensitive, complaint-driven, exception-driven, or a handoff.

workflow_stages.name: Card, certification, order, and pricing evidence retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Review card state, patient certification, MMAP status, POS totals, ETS activity, inventory availability, and receipt history before stating next steps or releasing an order disposition.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Supported request resolution and bounded retail communication
workflow_stages.description: Respond using only documented patient, caregiver, certification, pricing, order, and product-information state without promising clinical recommendations, pricing overrides, or unsupported returns.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Complaint, repeat-failure, and adverse-event escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package recurring MMAP, pricing, order, complaint, or adverse-event issues with traceable evidence and the correct next owner instead of retrying unsupported fixes.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Recall, clinical, and policy-conflict boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when recall status, adverse-event facts, program rules, or tenant policy materially change the safe answer and avoid representing the lane as licensed clinical or compliance authority.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: patient and caregiver registry records, patient ID card state, and caregiver linkage artifacts
patient certification status, limits on form or dosage, and remaining quantity or 90-day supply context
order, cart, receipt, POS pricing, MMAP benefit, and reimbursement-support artifacts
product sku, label, safety insert, THC/CBD, lot or batch, expiration, and recall-hold records
ETS and seed-to-sale activity, inventory availability, and release-blocker logs
complaint records, adverse-event reports, communication logs, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: Department database and electronic tracking system (ETS)
complaint, quality, and recall tracking systems

allowed: Classify patient-service work as in-scope, blocked, handoff-ready, or unresolved based on documented patient, caregiver, certification, pricing, inventory, complaint, and recall artifacts.
Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, retail workflow summaries, patient communication summaries, and escalation packets without claiming clinical, compliance, or pricing-approval authority.
Request or validate the minimum tenant facts, systems, and source records needed to continue safely.
escalate: Conflicting public guidance, jurisdiction rules, or tenant policy that materially change whether the product may be released, discounted, returned, or communicated as safe to use.
Requests requiring licensed medical consultation, compliance interpretation, recall or quality disposition, pricing approval, or security-sensitive handling.
Repeated patient-service failures where blocker history shows the issue is not being resolved in the current lane.
refuse: Issue clinical dosage advice, patient certification changes, or medical-use appropriateness recommendations.
Approve quantity overrides, price waivers, free product, home delivery, or unsupported return or refund exceptions.
Treat cannabis and controlled substance retail patient services as generic retail support that ignores patient card, caregiver, certification, MMAP, lot, or recall evidence.

domain_failure_modes: patient-service work proceeds without patient, caregiver, certification, or facility identifiers that materially change the answer
generic retail language replaces cannabis-specific records such as patient ID card, certification, MMAP benefit, medical marijuana units, safety insert, lot, or recall hold
pricing and MMAP issues are treated as discretionary discount decisions instead of documented point-of-sale and policy workflows
clinical product-use, dosage, or adverse-event questions are answered as ordinary retail support instead of being routed to licensed medical or quality owners
recall, complaint, or adverse-event cases are closed without Department, grower-processor, or corrective-action routing where required
registry, ETS, POS, and inventory conflicts are ignored instead of escalated as source-of-truth failures

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when the industry name cannot be treated as cosmetic
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdictions differ on whether patient-facing retail work is medical-only, hybrid medical-adult-use, or otherwise integrated with broader cannabis retail models.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change pickup rules, home-delivery availability, who may enter the facility, and what patient identity artifacts are mandatory.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Treat jurisdiction, facility, and program model as required tenant facts; use the Pennsylvania medical-dispensary workflow as the public baseline and escalate whenever another jurisdiction's rules materially change the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Operators implement different POS, seed-to-sale, MMAP, and recall workflows across facilities and vendors.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Could change which system is authoritative for price, available inventory, release status, or complaint routing.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require tenant system-of-record mapping and owner routing before resolving pricing, inventory, recall, or repeat-failure disputes.

notes: The research set intentionally combines Pennsylvania medical-marijuana program guidance, dispensary regulations, patient workflow guidance, MMAP retail workflow guidance, and ASTM cannabis standards so workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling are all covered.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated pack because variable jurisdiction and tenant workflow areas are converted into explicit tenant-fact and retrieval prerequisites.
